https://openalex.org/W2115452265.content:
The CORDIC Trigonometric Computing Technique

The COordinate Rotation DIgital Computer(CORDIC) is a special-purpose digital computer for real-time airborne computation. https://openalex.org/W2140153041.content:
GMRES: A Generalized Minimal Residual Algorithm for Solving Nonsymmetric Linear Systems

We present an iterative method for solving linear systems, which has the property of minimizing at every step norm residual vector over a Krylov subspace. The algorithm is derived from Arnoldi process constructing $l_2 $-orthogonal basis subspaces. It can be considered as generalization Paige and Saunders’ MINRES theoretically equivalent to Generalized Conjugate Residual (GCR) ORTHODIR. new presents several advantages GCR

Recently the Conjugate Gradients-Squared (CG-S) method has been proposed as an attractive variant of Bi-Conjugate Gradients (Bi-CG) method. Lattice BGK Models for Navier-Stokes Equation

We propose the lattice BGK models, as an alternative to gases or Boltzmann equation, obtain efficient numerical scheme for simulation of fluid dynamics. With a properly chosen equilibrium distribution, Navier-Stokes equation is obtained from kinetic at second-order approximation. Compared gases, present model noise-free, has Galileian invariance and velocity-independent pressure. It involves relaxation parameter that influences stability new scheme. Numerical simulations are shown confirm speed sound shear viscosity.

Gradient Projection for Sparse Reconstruction: Application to Compressed Sensing and Other Inverse Problems

Many problems in signal processing and statistical inference involve finding sparse solutions to under-determined, or ill-conditioned, linear systems of equations. A standard approach consists minimizing an objective function which includes a quadratic (squared ) error term combined with sparseness-inducing regularization term. Basis pursuit, the least absolute shrinkage selection operator (LASSO), wavelet-based deconvolution, compressed sensing are few well-known examples this approach. This paper proposes gradient projection (GP) algorithms for bound-constrained programming (BCQP) formulation these problems. We test variants that select line search parameters different ways, including techniques based on Barzilai-Borwein method. Computational experiments show GP approaches perform well wide range applications, often being significantly faster (in terms computation time) than competing methods. The traditional SPM approach based on bag-of-features (BoF) requires nonlinear classifiers to achieve good image classification performance. From the original PREFACE: The rings of quotients recently introduced by Johnson and Utumi are applied to ring $C(X)$ all continuous real-valued functions on a completely regular space $X$. Let $Q(X)$ denote maximal $C(X)$; then may be realized as dense open sets $X$ (modulo an obvious equivalence relation). In special cases (e.g., for metric $X$), reduces classical (formed with respect elements), but in general, is only proper sub-ring $Q(X)$.

The article discusses the application of rings of quotients, as introduced by Johnson and Utumi, to the ring of continuous real-valued functions on completely regular spaces, specifically focusing on the structure and properties of the maximal ring \( Q(X) \) and its relationship to classical function spaces in various mathematical contexts.
